things.name: Kilkenny Castle
things.description: Magnificent 12th-century Norman castle dominating the River Nore — spectacular state rooms, a Victorian picture gallery and beautiful grounds

things.name: St Canice's Cathedral & Round Tower
things.description: Ireland's second-longest medieval cathedral (1251) with rich carved monuments — climb the 9th-century round tower for panoramic views over the city

things.name: Medieval Mile Museum
things.description: Ireland's Museum of the Year in the magnificent St Mary's Church — underground passageways and 600 years of Kilkenny's medieval story

things.name: Rothe House & Gardens
things.description: Ireland's best-preserved Tudor merchant's townhouse (1594) with a reconstructed Renaissance garden and folk museum

things.name: Kilkenny Design Centre
things.description: Ireland's finest design showroom in the former castle stables — hand-selected Irish crafts, jewellery, clothing and gifts

things.name: Black Abbey Dominican Church
things.description: Medieval friary church founded 1225 with remarkable 14th-century alabaster carvings and a stunning Victorian stained-glass window

things.name: Jerpoint Abbey (Thomastown)
things.description: One of Ireland's finest Cistercian abbeys (1158) with ornately carved cloister arcades and Ireland's most significant collection of medieval tombs

things.name: Dunmore Cave (UNESCO Geopark)
things.description: Spectacular limestone cave system with massive calcite formations and a dark history — the site of a Viking massacre in 928 AD

things.name: Kilkenny Arts Festival (August)
things.description: Ireland's foremost multi-disciplinary arts festival transforming Kilkenny's medieval streetscapes every August with world-class events

things.name: Rock of Cashel (Day Trip)
things.description: Extraordinary 12th-century cathedral complex on a dramatic limestone rock — the ecclesiastical capital of Munster
